---
title: Quick Start
---

## Access and getting Started
### Requesting project space
If your research project does not have a project space yet, [request it](cost_access.qmd#how-to-request-access) first. A Yoda functional admin will create the project space and invite you as a Group Manager.

### Inviting colleagues
Once a project space is created a user with the Group Manager role can [invite colleagues](group_manager.qmd) and set their [access rights](group_manager.qmd#how-to-changing-the-role-of-a-user-in-a-group). 

### Logging in
VU users can [login](logging_in.qmd) straight away after being added, with their primary email address as username. 

Newly added *external* users receive an [invitation](sram_invitations_eduid_activation.qmd), after accepting they can [login](logging_in.qmd) with their institutional account or EduID, with their primary e-mail address as username. 

## Working With Your Project Space
A project space is a data folder for storing and organising data during your project. It has a structured name: `research-<faculty>-<department>-<project>`.

### Data Transfer
Depending on the Operating System you are using, different methods can be used to [transfer data](data_access_instructions.qmd) to and from your data folder. 

### Working with analysis tools
Although you cannot directly access the data in Yoda with analysis tools, there are [several ways](analysing_data.qmd) to work with your data. 

### Searching
You can easily [search](finding_restoring.qmd) for your files in Yoda using the built-in search tool.

## Archiving and Publishing
Yoda enables you to [add metadata](workflow_metadata.qmd) to describe and contextualise your dataset. 

Once your research is complete, you can [archive](vault_archive.qmd) and [publish](vault_publish.qmd) it, so you can reference it with a DOI.
